message:
chore: remove stray compiled binaries from repo root

Two compiled ELF executables left over from ad-hoc compile/run cycles
should never have been tracked. Both are reproducible from source via
the workspace's normal cargo build flow:

- rust_out: 4.4 MB stripped-but-unstripped binary, output of an earlier
  cargo build --release dropped at the workspace root rather than into
  target/.
- test_ptr: 4.3 MB binary from a one-off test of the ptrmap scaffolding
  (matching test_sqlite_ptrmap.c, removed in a follow-up commit).

Both are covered by the existing /target/ + a.out + binary-shaped
.gitignore rules going forward; the only reason they linger in working
trees is that they were committed before those rules existed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
